title: Peel and stick roofing membranes with cured pressure-sensitive adhesives and expandable graphite
abstract: A membrane composite comprising a polymeric membrane panel, an adhesive layer, where the adhesive layer is a pressure-sensitive adhesive that is at least partially cured, expandable graphite, and a release member.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A membrane composite comprising: a. a polymeric membrane panel; b. an adhesive layer, where the adhesive layer is a pressure-sensitive adhesive that is at least partially cured; c. expandable graphite; and d. a release member. 2. The membrane composite of claim 1, where the adhesive layer is in contact with substantially all of one planar surface of the membrane panel. 3. The membrane composite of any of the preceding claims, where the adhesive layer includes a UV-cured poly(acrylate) resin. 4. The membrane composite of any of the preceding claims, where the polymeric membrane is a thermoplastic membrane. 5. The membrane composite of any of the preceding claims, where the polymeric membrane is a cured rubber membrane. 6. The membrane composite of any of the preceding claims, where the membrane composite is characterized by a peel strength, according to PSTC 101, of at least 3.0 psi. 7. The membrane composite of any of the preceding claims, where the membrane composite is characterized by a dead load shear, according to PSTC 107, of at least 0.5 hour (time of failure). 8. The membrane composite of any of the preceding claims, where the adhesive layer has a thickness of from about 10 to about 381 Î¼ÏÎ¹. 9. The membrane composite of any of the preceding claims, where the expandable graphite is dispersed within said adhesive layer. 10. The membrane composite of any of the preceding claims, where the expandable graphite is included within a concentrated region of expandable graphite proximate to said adhesive layer. 11. A process for forming a membrane composite, the process comprising: a. preparing a blend of a melt-extrudable, UV-curable, pressure-sensitive adhesive and expandable graphite; b. heating the blend of the melt-extrudable, UV-curable, pressure-sensitive adhesive and expandable graphite allowing the blend to flow; c. applying the blend to a planar surface of a membrane panel to form a coating of adhesive; d. subjecting the coating of the adhesive to UV radiation to thereby effect crosslinking of the adhesive; e. applying a release member to the adhesive coating to form a composite; and f. winding the composite. 12. The process of any of the preceding claims, where said step of heating heats the adhesive to a temperature of from about 120 to about 160 Â°C. 13. The process of any of the preceding claims, where said step of applying the adhesive forms a coating layer having a thickness of from about 10 to about 381 Î¼ÏÎ¹. The process of any of the preceding claims, where the expandable graphite has onset temperature of less than 180 Â°C. The process of any of the preceding claims, where said step of subjecting the coating to UV radiation includes subjecting the adhesive to a UV dosage of from about 50 to about 230 millijoules/cm^. The process of any of the preceding claims, where said step of subjecting the coating to UV radiation includes subjecting the adhesive to at least 150 milliWatts/cm2 of UV energy. The process of any of the preceding claims, where said step of subjecting the coating to UV radiation includes subjecting the adhesive to at least 200 milliWatts/cm2 of UV energy. The process of any of the preceding claims, where the adhesive includes more units deriving from butyl acrylate or 2-ethylhexyl acrylate. 19. The process of any of the preceding claims, where the adhesive has a Tg of less than 0 Â°C. The process of any of the preceding claims, where said step of subjecting the coating of the adhesive to UV radiation takes place at a temperature of less than 180 Â°C.
